response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with deep web, backend, and systems experience, but the core mandatory requirements are not met: his C++ experience is limited to a ~6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis project, far short of the 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ required. He has no Fixed Income, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain experience — RIDE Capital was a retail investment-adjacent product built in Node/TypeScript, not capital markets systems. SQL exposure appears to be MySQL/MongoDB rather than SQL Server/Oracle.
strengths: Solid low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang async I/O at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering fundamentals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and testing culture
Broad polyglot background with proven ability to pick up new stacks quickly
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~6 months professionally, ended 2015), with no Windows-platform focus
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain exposure
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary databases are MongoDB and MySQL

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and low-latency systems background
